title: ChatGPT handoff inbox and MCP facade architecture
summary: Design and implement the next repo-harness control-plane slice: a Handoff Inbox for ChatGPT continuation, a thin ChatGPT-facing MCP facade, capability routing, and policy-gated execution so plugin capabilities remain parallel to repository operations without expanding ChatGPT's visible tool surface.
goals: Add a durable Handoff Inbox concept that lets repo-harness record pending decisions for ChatGPT/main-controller continuation.
Define a small ChatGPT-facing facade tool surface for status, inbox, context, work, and evidence-oriented operations.
Keep repository operations and plugin capabilities parallel internally, routed through capability/policy layers instead of exposing one MCP tool per feature.
Avoid unsafe or security-flag-prone request shapes by using typed operations, bounded summaries, policy gates, approval requests, and suggested next actions.
nonGoals: Do not replace ChatGPT as the strong主控.
Do not build a low-level clone of Codex.
Do not expand the public ChatGPT-facing tool list for each new capability.
Do not modify unrelated browser plugin changes currently dirty on main.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture documentation explains how plugin capabilities are parallel to repository execution capabilities and how both route through a facade/capability/policy model.
A first implementation slice exists for Handoff Inbox and/or facade contracts with bounded result shapes.
ChatGPT-facing operations expose stable, limited entry points rather than new per-feature tools.
Security and safety considerations are documented: bounded payloads, no raw shell blobs by default, explicit approval for risky operations, and no prompt shapes that ask ChatGPT to bypass security.
Targeted checks or type checks are run where available, and the existing unrelated dirty main changes are not overwritten.

tasks.title: Document handoff inbox and MCP facade architecture

Docs define a stable ChatGPT-facing facade surface and explain why new capabilities should register internally instead of becoming new tools.
Docs include safety/security request-shape guidance for ChatGPT-facing operations.
Docs include a staged implementation plan.

tasks.title: Implement handoff/facade MVP contracts
tasks.objective: Add the smallest viable code slice for Handoff Inbox and MCP facade/result contracts, reusing existing controller storage and avoiding broad runtime changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Handoff item and unified facade result contracts are represented in code.
The design supports bounded summary/detail behavior and evidence refs.
Facade contracts avoid adding one ChatGPT-facing tool per capability.
Tests or targeted validation cover the new contracts where practical.

acceptanceResults.evidence: Added src/runtime/control-plane/facade/types.ts with HandoffItem, FacadeResult, SuggestedNextAction, WorkContract, PolicyDecision, CapabilityDescriptor, and execution mode contracts.

acceptanceResults.criterion: The design supports bounded summary/detail behavior and evidence refs.
acceptanceResults.evidence: FacadeResult includes detailLevel, evidenceRefs, rawAvailable, warnings, and suggestedNextActions; HandoffItem references bounded evidenceRefs rather than raw logs.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Tests or targeted validation cover the new contracts where practical.

tasks.title: Implement handoff inbox MVP store
tasks.objective: Add a minimal controller-home-backed Handoff Inbox store for creating, listing, reading, acknowledging, and resolving handoff items using the facade contracts without changing existing direct edit, MCP routing, or plugin behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Handoff Inbox store persists under controller-home repository runtime storage or an injected root in tests.
Store supports create/list/get/acknowledge/resolve operations with bounded HandoffItem contracts.
List defaults to pending active items and does not return raw logs.
Targeted tests cover create/list/get/acknowledge/resolve behavior and typecheck passes.
notes: Verification evidence passed and medium_risk_change policy auto-completed the Task.

commandEvidence.stdout: Passed via edit session verification: package:check:type / bun run check:type.
10 pass, 0 fail, 21 expect() calls. Ran 10 tests across 2 files.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Handoff Inbox store persists under controller-home repository runtime storage or an injected root in tests.
acceptanceResults.evidence: handoff-inbox-store.ts writes index.json under repositoryControllerRoot(controllerHome, repoId)/handoff-inbox, or an injected root for tests.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Store supports create/list/get/acknowledge/resolve operations with bounded HandoffItem contracts.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Implemented createHandoffItem, listHandoffItems, getHandoffItem, acknowledgeHandoffItem, and resolveHandoffItem using HandoffItem/HandoffInboxStore contracts.

acceptanceResults.criterion: List defaults to pending active items and does not return raw logs.
acceptanceResults.evidence: listHandoffItems defaults to status pending, supports active/all, and returns HandoffItem objects with evidenceRefs rather than raw logs.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Targeted tests cover create/list/get/acknowledge/resolve behavior and typecheck passes.
